Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Input An event log with 5 cases C001 C005 each going through a process ApplicationReceived DataValidation PreliminaryScoring ManualReview FinalDecision Attributes CaseID Activity Timestamp LocalResident CommunityGroup PreliminaryScore ScoreAdjustment ManualReview Decision Resource Question Identify where and how bias manifests in this process Which attributes and adjustments favor certain groups How might that influence fairness equity Consider implications for individuals lacking certain community affiliations or geographic characteristics even with similar creditworthiness

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Only cases with CommunityGroup Highland Civic Darts Club get a 10 Community adjustment Others get 0

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: All cases with the community group are also LocalResident TRUE Cases without it are mixed C002 is TRUE C003 C005 are FALSE

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: C003 Score 715 LocalResident FALSE Community None Rejected C004 Score 690 plus 10 equals 700 LocalResident TRUE Community Highland Approved So a lower scoring applicant 700 is approved over a higher scoring applicant 715 purely due to community affiliation local residency

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This strongly suggests that the CommunityGroup affiliation and possibly LocalResident status is directly influencing the decision overriding or interacting with the score in a biased way

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: C005 Score 740 LocalResident FALSE Community None Approved This shows that high scores can overcome the lack of affiliation but the threshold for non affiliated applicants appears higher

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The ScoreAdjustment column explicitly shows 10 Community for Highland Civic Darts Club members This is a direct systematic bonus based on group membership

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: LocalResident might also play a role but the log explicitly ties the adjustment to Community However all community members are also local residents suggesting possible intersectional bias or proxy discrimination

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: The 10 Community score adjustment directly favors members of a specific community group Highland Civic Darts Club This is a non meritocratic factor unrelated to creditworthiness

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The approval threshold appears lower for community members local residents C004 700 adjusted is approved while C003 715 is rejected This means non members need a higher baseline score to achieve the same outcome

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This could indicate geographic bias or insider favoritism where local community ties are rewarded over objective financial metrics

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The ManualReview step does not seem to correct or question the adjustment Underwriters simply pass through the adjusted scores The Rules Engine at FinalDecision appears to encode this bias into automated approvals rejections

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: The process prioritizes social geographic affiliation over financial risk Someone with a lower score 690 to 700 gets approved over someone with a higher score 715 purely due to club membership

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: Individuals not part of the specific community or not local residents face a higher effective threshold This creates systemic inequality especially if the community group is exclusive historically homogeneous or hard to join

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: Using non financial social affiliations for scoring adjustments can violate fair lending principles While darts club is not itself a protected class it could act as a proxy for protected characteristics like race ethnicity or socioeconomic status

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Reinforces in group advantage limits access for outsiders newcomers and could lead to adverse selection if lower creditworthy insiders are approved while higher creditworthy outsiders are rejected

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Check against data C001 710 plus 10 equals 720 Approved C002 720 Approved C003 715 Rejected C004 690 plus 10 equals 700 Approved C005 740 Approved Threshold analysis Non members seem to need above 715 since 715 rejected 720 and 740 approved Members get approved at 700 This is a clear disparate impact

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait lets double check C002 LocalResident TRUE Community None Score 720 Approved So local residency alone does not guarantee the 10 but it might lower the threshold slightly Actually C002 and C001 both approved at 720 The key differentiator is the 10 adjustment for the club The threshold for non club seems to be above 715 For club members it is at or below 700 I will stick to the community group as the primary bias driver with local residency as a correlated factor
